items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation.

items.title: Improper Encoding/Escaping of Output Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution via a crafted cookie.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This security update addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, which if exploited, could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot technique to bypass validation.
